openFindings.summary: The anti-slop vocabulary reference points to a nonexistent path.
openFindings.message: The document directs readers to `sk-design/references/anti-slop-principles.md`, which does not exist. The live parent contract and file inventory identify `.opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/anti-slop-principles.md` as the canonical shared reference.

openFindings.summary: The frontmatter overstates fingerprint tells as proof despite the document's hypothesis-only rule.
openFindings.message: The frontmatter says the catalog contains tells that prove a surface was AI-generated, while the evidence contract says the page-furniture tells are hypotheses and do not prove a generator. Because sk-doc defines `description` as the document's human-readable purpose, the metadata must preserve the evidence boundary expressed by the body.
The live sk-doc validator reports “Missing required section: overview”; after the H1 the document proceeds directly into prose and a code block, while its only H2 is “References.”
The live sk-doc structure extractor scores the document 67/100, below the authority's 75-point floor; the document has only one H2 and lacks the expected reference-document structure.
Missing required section: overview
Missing required section: overview
Four catch-block comments begin with lowercase text, contradicting sk-code's mandatory comment-capitalization rule.

crawl.ts is 1,043 lines and combines crawling, browser lifecycle, consent handling, link prioritization, interaction triggering, screenshots, and throttling. This substantially exceeds sk-code's 400-line recommendation even when treated as a main entry point; extract domain modules.

css-analyzer.ts is 563 lines and combines stylesheet acquisition, selector/declaration parsing, media queries, transitions, animations, and aggregate analysis. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; split by analysis domain.
dark-mode-detect.ts is 266 lines, exceeding sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation. Separate detection/switching from variable and screenshot capture if this module continues growing.

design-boundary-detect.ts is 311 lines and combines six scoring dimensions, anomaly detection, summaries, pair aggregation, and classification. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; extract scoring helpers by concern.

openFindings.summary: DOM collector exceeds the utility-module length guideline
openFindings.message: dom-collector.ts is 575 lines and combines CSS-variable, computed-style, pseudo-element, gradient, SVG, font, and logo-color extraction. It exceeds sk-code's 200-line utility recommendation; split the independent collectors into focused modules.

icon-detect.ts is 248 lines, exceeding sk-code's 200-line recommendation for utility/helper modules. Split page-dependent label detection or distribution helpers into a focused module.

interaction-capture.ts is 799 lines, nearly four times sk-code's 200-line recommendation for utility/helper modules. Extract the independent state-detection and interaction-capture concerns into focused modules.

The strict TypeScript gate reports TS2345 because a string|null value is passed where string is required; this is an artifact-local type-system conformance failure and must be resolved before the package meets the sk-code TypeScript standard.
openFindings.evidence: [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-md-generator/backend/scripts/report-gen.ts:694] `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` reports TS2345: Argument of type 'string | null' is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.

openFindings.summary: Node built-in import omits the node: prefix
openFindings.message: The validator imports the Node built-in as 'fs' rather than the OpenCode surface's node:fs convention, which is already followed by the sibling study preparation module.

openFindings.summary: Property explanations use trailing comments
openFindings.message: StabilityClassification places explanatory comments after property declarations; the authority's commenting convention places explanatory comments on the preceding line.
